name: tdd-orchestrator
description: Master TDD orchestrator specializing in red-green-refactor
capabilities: Orchestrate complete red-green-refactor TDD cycles with enforcement mechanisms
Coordinate multi-agent testing workflows across unit, integration, and E2E levels
Guide implementation of classic London and Chicago TDD schools
Provide AI-assisted test generation strategies and best practices
Design test suite architecture including pyramid optimization and categorization
Establish TDD metrics and quality gates for team compliance monitoring
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Prompt-only skill containing no executable code. Provides guidance on TDD practices and testing methodologies. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines of code. No security risks detected. This is an informational/educational skill about software testing best practices.
valueStatement: This skill helps development teams implement disciplined test-driven development practices through comprehensive workflow orchestration and AI-assisted testing guidance.
limitations: Does not execute or write actual test code - provides guidance only
Does not integrate with specific IDEs or build tools directly
Does not run test suites or provide real-time feedback on code
Requires user to implement recommended practices in their environment

useCases.targetUser: Software developers starting a new project
useCases.title: New Project TDD Setup
useCases.description: Establish TDD discipline and workflow for a new software project with proper test pyramid structure

useCases.title: Legacy Code Testing Strategy
useCases.description: Create comprehensive test coverage strategy for existing legacy codebase using characterization tests

useCases.title: Cross-Team TDD Governance
useCases.description: Implement organization-wide TDD standards with compliance verification and metrics collection

promptTemplates.title: Basic TDD Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me implement TDD for a new feature. Guide me through the red-green-refactor cycle for [describe your feature].

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Agent Testing Workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a multi-agent workflow for coordinated testing across unit, integration, and E2E levels for [describe project].

promptTemplates.title: TDD Metrics Dashboard
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a TDD metrics framework including cycle time, coverage thresholds, and quality gates for [describe team size].

promptTemplates.title: Property-Based Testing Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement property-based testing strategy using [framework] for complex business logic validation in [describe domain].

bestPractices: Write the smallest possible test that fails before writing any implementation code
Keep refactoring focused only on making code cleaner without changing behavior
Maintain fast test execution times to preserve rapid feedback cycles
antiPatterns: Writing tests after implementation code (test-after is not TDD)
Testing implementation details rather than observable behavior
Creating overly complex tests that are hard to maintain and debug

faq.question: What programming languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill provides guidance for multiple languages including Java, C#, Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, and Go with their respective testing frameworks.

faq.question: How long does it take to adopt TDD in a team?
faq.answer: Initial adoption typically takes 2-4 weeks of consistent practice. Mastery of TDD discipline usually requires 3-6 months of dedicated application.

faq.question: Can this skill help with legacy code?
faq.answer: Yes, it provides strategies for characterization tests, golden master testing, and incremental TDD adoption for existing codebases.

faq.question: Does this skill integrate with CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: The skill provides guidance on pipeline design but does not directly integrate. It can recommend pipeline configurations for TDD compliance.

faq.question: What is the difference between TDD and BDD?
faq.answer: TDD focuses on developer tests written to specify behavior. BDD uses natural language scenarios (Given-When-Then) to define behavior from user perspective.

faq.question: How do I measure TDD success in my team?
faq.answer: Track metrics including cycle time, test coverage percentage, defect escape rate, and refactoring frequency. The skill provides detailed metrics framework guidance.
